title: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes
display_title: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes
wikitext:
[]: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Oct 19, 2009 12:51 am In particular, while perusing TVTropes, I came to the The Walrus Was Paul page and noticed this part: TVTropes wrote: * Evangelion is the very DEFINITION of this trope. You'd think that after stunts such as Gendo's infamously silenced line in The End of Evangelion it would be very clear that Hideaki Anno was intentionally going for this, yet still a vast number of "Eva fans" absolutely refuse to accept that Evangelion does not have any single, all-encompassing, absolute meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Mon Oct 19, 2009 1:08 am Well...I think what they were tying to say with Gendo's silent line was that Anno chose to have it be silent as a way to fuck with the viewer. I gotta say I agree with the troper. Yojimbo, I, and others believe we figured it out to be "I truly have no regrets concerning my work"

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Oct 19, 2009 1:56 am Captain_Morgan wrote: Yojimbo, I, and others believe we figured it out to be "I truly have no regrets concerning my work"

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Oct 19, 2009 2:20 am Monk Ed wrote: the trope itself seems to refer more to interpretations of meaning (like whether or not Fuyutsuki represents the Wandering Jew)

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Mon Oct 19, 2009 2:26 am The Christian symbols would go better in "What Do You Mean, It's Not Symbolic?" The Walrus Was Paul seemed to me like the writers deliberately trying to fuck with the audience. In this case, the silent line became a silent line to mess with the viewers who try to find out the absolute answers and meanings to every little thing about the show. Did you and Yojimbo let the fact that we don't know what Gendo said get to you? In a way, yes. You went looking for an answer. Have you found an "answer?" Yes. Will we ever know if it's the right "answer?" Nope. Yes, this probably isn't the best example since it can be said that there is an answer as to what Gendo said. However, since it is unlikely we will ever get an answer, it might as well not have an answer. In a way, Anno was poking fun at or even saying "fuck you" (since this is EoE) to those who look into things too much, since he probably knew that people would obsess over this one little line that never got recorded. Also, I don't recall the troper claiming Anno said there was no line, only that they intentionally threw in random imagery and other crap to mindfuck the viewers.

[]: Re: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:16 pm Monk Ed wrote: Since TVTropes is not just some kid's random website but seems to be respected to a degree, shouldn't it be corrected?

[]: Re: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes?             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:26 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Bottom line; I think hushing that line was more about Anno keeping something to himself than it was screwing with the fans.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:31 pm You (slightly) misunderstood me Xard; I don't disagree with anything you said in that I definitely think the metafictional angle played into it too, yet there's a reason he hushed that line of all the hundreds of lines he could've hushed in the film, and if that line is what I think it is then my explanation makes eminent sense to me. Further, I was mostly attempting to explain why I don't think that line falls under the mindfuck trope.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:38 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: You (slightly) misunderstood me Xard; I don't disagree with anything you said in that I definitely think the metafictional angle played into it too, yet there's a reason he hushed that line of all the hundreds of lines he could've hushed in the film, and if that line is what I think it is then my explanation makes eminent sense to me. Further, I was mostly attempting to explain why I don't think that line falls under the mindfuck trope.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:43 pm Xard wrote: Admitting feeling like all human race could as well die seems to me far more "controversial" feeling than anything Gendo could've possibly told to Ritsuko

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Oct 19, 2009 5:46 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Xard wrote: Admitting feeling like all human race could as well die seems to me far more "controversial" feeling than anything Gendo could've possibly told to Ritsuko

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Oct 19, 2009 6:29 pm I don't know if I consider Shinji's fapping scene an Anno analog at all. I definitely agree that there are parts of Shinji (and all of the characters, really) that act as Anno analogs, I think Shinji's fapping is perhaps the most obvious example of his cultural criticism that's not attached to himself (or if it is we'll never know). It's not that saying that line would've "hurt him" it's just that... well, it's a bit hard to explain. As I said, I think the allegorical nature of NGE gave Anno a cushion and even when it was more direct (EoTV) it was still about the big/abstract concepts. I still see a personalness in that line that justifies (or, at least, provides reason for) the hushing.

[]: Re: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes?             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Oct 20, 2009 1:16 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Alaska Slim wrote: It has an underlying guideline to that meaning, an objective Hideaki Anno himself has admitted to, and the other creators have seconded on, that being, getting the otaku to wake up.

[]: Re: Anybody ever thought of correcting TVTropes?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Oct 20, 2009 3:17 pm Alaska Slim wrote: However, their original motive for addressing all of this remains the same, and even if others can share in that, it is imperative that the motive kept in mind.
